strands.title: Skill Category 1: Skill Category 1: Rhetorical Situation – Reading
Skill Category 2: Skill Category 2: Rhetorical Situation – Writing
Skill Category 3: Skill Category 3: Claims and Evidence – Reading
Skill Category 4: Skill Category 4: Claims and Evidence – Writing
Skill Category 5: Skill Category 5: Reasoning and Organization – Reading
Skill Category 6: Skill Category 6: Reasoning and Organization – Writing
Skill Category 7: Skill Category 7: Style – Reading
Skill Category 8: Skill Category 8: Style – Writing
Identify and explain claims and evidence within arguments.
Identify and describe rhetorical situation components including exigence, audience, writer, purpose, context, message.
Write introductions and conclusions appropriate to rhetorical situation purpose and context.
Develop paragraphs including claims with supporting evidence.
Describe line of reasoning and assess support for argument's overarching thesis.
Develop line of reasoning with explanatory commentary throughout arguments.
Explain how stylistic choices contribute to argument purpose.
Strategically use words, comparisons, and syntax to convey specific tone or style.
Write sentences that clearly convey ideas and arguments.
Explain how text organization creates unity, coherence, and reflects reasoning.
Explain how clause placement and combination show relationships among ideas.
Demonstrate understanding of audience beliefs, values, and needs in writing.
Explain how arguments demonstrate understanding of audience beliefs, values, and needs.
Identify and describe overarching thesis and structural indicators within arguments.
Use transitional elements to guide reader through argument's line of reasoning.
Write thesis statements requiring proof and potentially previewing argument structure.
